content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
April 6, 1988


Honorable Helen S. Kawagoe
City Clerk
City of Carson
Post Office Box 6234
Carson, CA  90749

Re:  Your Request for Advice;
Our File No. I-88-111

Dear Ms. Kawagoe:

This is a follow-up letter to my previous response to your
request for advice regarding the application of Government Code
Section 89001 to mass mailings by local elected officers who
have filed for election.

At its regular meeting yesterday, the Fair Political
Practices Commission adopted revisions to 2 California Code of
Regulations Section 18001.  The revised regulation provides the
Commission's interpretation of the statute to guide elected
officers in complying with the law.  I enclose a copy of the
new regulation for your review.  The Commission selected the
proposed alternative #3, with the exception of subdivision
(d)(4)(F), which was deleted.  That subdivision would have
permitted the sending of appropriate holiday greetings.

I also enclose a copy of a chart which the staff prepared
for the Commission's consideration in adopting the changes in
the regulation.  With the exception of the holiday greetings
provision which has been removed, the description of
Alternative #3 contained in the chart should be of assistance
to you in determining which types of mass mailings are
prohibited.  You should note that the depiction of an incumbent
candidate in any photograph is expressly prohibited during the
period in which the statute applies.  (See Government Code
Section 89001.)

I trust that this responds to your request for guidance as
to what is permissible under the statute.  Should you have
specific questions, I may be reached at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,


Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel, Legal Division

REL:plh
Enclosure
cc:  Steve Dorsey

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 22, 1988

Helen S. Kawagoe
City Clerk
City of Carson
P.O. Box 6234
Carson, CA 90749

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-88-111

Dear Ms. Kawagoe:

Your letter of March 14 to Jeanne Pritchard has been
forwarded to me for a response. You have asked this agency to
advise you whether the March 1988 "Carson Report" city
newsletter was sent in violation of 2 California Code of
Regulations Section 18901. The Commission does not give advice
regarding past conduct. (See 2 Cal. Code of Regs. Section
18329(b)(8)(A), copy enclosed.)

The regulation about which you have inquired deals with
newsletters or mass mailings sent by or on behalf of incumbents
who have filed for election. As currently worded, the
regulation does not specifically address mailings made by local
government agencies. However, the Commission is considering
amending the regulation to specify which types of local
government mailings are permitted or prohibited.

At its next meeting, April 5, 1988 in Sacramento, the
Commission will consider adoption of a revised regulation from
among four alternatives which have been noticed for its
consideration. Copies of those alternatives are enclosed for
your review and comment. Your comments and those of the City
of Carson would be welcome. If you have questions, I may be
reached at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Robert E. Laidigh
Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosures

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

CITY CLERK

FPP'
MAR 17 8 47 AH 88

CITY OF CARSON

March 14, 1988

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Political Reform Consultant
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA    95804

Dear Jeanne:

As I discussed with you by telephone this date, I am forwarding to you a copy of the most recent CARSON REPORT, published and mailed by the City of Carson to its residents.

The question that has been posed to the City Clerk is "Is this mailing a violation of California Code of Regulations, Section 18901?"

Please review and advise as soon as possible. Your past cooperation is appreciated. Your present support from the department is excellent. Thank you!

Sincerely,

Helen S. Kawagoe, CMC
City Clerk

Enclosures (3)

CITY HALL • 701 EAST CARSON STREET • P.O. BOX 6234 • CARSON, CALIFORNIA 90749 • (213) 830-7600

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
March 17, 1988


Helen S. Kawagoe, CMC
City Clerk
City of Carson
P.O. Box 6234
Carson, CA 90749

Re: 88-111

Dear Ms. Kawagoe:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on March 17, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact Robert Leidigh, an attorney in
the Legal Division, directly at (916) 322-5901.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to information needed. If your request is for
informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we can.
(See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec.
18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

March 14, 1988


Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Political Reform Consultant
Fair Political Practices Commission
P. O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA    95804

Dear Jeanne:

As I discussed with you by telephone this date, I am forwarding
to you a copy of the most recent CARSON REPORT, published and mailed
by the City of Carson to its residents.

The question that has been posed to the City Clerk is "Is this mailing

sections.question: Is the March 1988 'Carson Report' city newsletter sent by the City of Carson in violation of 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18901?
sections.conclusion: The Commission does not give advice regarding past conduct. However, the Commission is considering amending the regulation to specify which types of local government mailings are permitted or prohibited. The Commission adopted revisions to 2 California Code of Regulations section 18901, selecting Alternative #3 (with exception of subdivision (d)(4)(F) regarding holiday greetings). Depiction of an incumbent candidate in any photograph is expressly prohibited during the period in which the statute applies.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a city newsletter published by the City of Carson constitutes a prohibited mass mailing under regulations governing incumbents who have filed for election.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declined to advise on the past newsletter but provided guidance through adopted regulatory revisions clarifying which types of local government mass mailings are permitted or prohibited under Government Code section 89001, with strict prohibition on incumbent candidate photographs.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Multiple letters included in document sequence (incoming request, acknowledgment letter, substantive response, and follow-up). Primary advice provided in March 22, 1988 and April 6, 1988 letters from FPPC Counsel. Document contains both formal advice letter characteristics and policy guidance on regulatory adoption.

CONCLUSION: The Commission does not give advice regarding past conduct. However, the Commission is considering amending the regulation to specify which types of local government mailings are permitted or prohibited. The Commission adopted revisions to 2 California Code of Regulations section 18901, selecting Alternative #3 (with exception of subdivision (d)(4)(F) regarding holiday greetings). Depiction of an incumbent candidate in any photograph is expressly prohibited during the period in which the statute applies.

embedding.summary: The City Clerk of Carson requested advice on whether a city newsletter violated FPPC regulations on mass mailings by incumbents who have filed for election. The FPPC declined to review past conduct but provided guidance through newly adopted regulatory revisions clarifying permissible and prohibited local government mailings.
